name: review-changes
description: Code review of current git changes, compare to related plan if exists, identify bad engineering, over-engineering, or suboptimal solutions. Use when user asks to review changes, check git diff, validate implementation quality, or assess code changes.
capabilities: Review git changes with git status, diff, and diff HEAD commands
Compare implementation against existing plans in .plans directory
Identify bad engineering patterns like bugs, missing error handling, and security issues
Detect over-engineering including unnecessary abstraction and premature optimization
Find suboptimal solutions such as code duplication and incorrect patterns
Verify 100 percent issue or task requirement coverage before approval
author: Cygnusfear
securitySummary: This is a pure prompt-based skill containing only instructional content for systematic code review. No executable code, no network calls, no file writes. All capabilities are inherent to the AI's built-in git and file reading tools. All 70 static findings are false positives triggered by documentation patterns, not actual security risks.
valueStatement: Manual code review is time-consuming and inconsistent. This skill provides a systematic framework for reviewing git changes, checking plan compliance, and identifying engineering issues with specific line numbers and actionable recommendations.
limitations: Does not modify code - review only, no implementation changes
Requires git repository with changes to review
Does not execute tests or verify functionality
Depends on AI file reading capabilities for detailed analysis

useCases.title: Pre-merge Code Review
useCases.description: Review pull request changes before merging to catch bugs, security issues, and quality problems early

useCases.title: Architecture Compliance Check
useCases.description: Verify implementation matches architectural plans and identify scope creep or deviation from design

useCases.title: Standard Enforcement
useCases.description: Systematically enforce coding standards, detect anti-patterns, and ensure consistency across codebase

promptTemplates.title: Basic Review
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the review-changes skill to review all current git changes in this repository

promptTemplates.title: Plan Comparison
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the review-changes skill to review changes and verify they match the plan in .plans directory

promptTemplates.title: Security Focus
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the review-changes skill with focus on security issues, bugs, and critical engineering problems

promptTemplates.title: Issue Coverage
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the review-changes skill to verify that all requirements from GitHub issue are implemented

bestPractices: Always verify issue or task coverage is 100 percent before approving changes
Reference specific file paths and line numbers for every issue found
Compare implementation against plans when available to catch scope creep
antiPatterns: Approving code without checking plan compliance or requirement coverage
Reviewing only some files and missing changed files in the diff
Not distinguishing between critical bugs and low-priority suggestions

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: This skill works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code. It uses built-in git and file reading capabilities.

faq.question: How many files can this skill review at once?
faq.answer: The skill reviews all changed files in the git diff. Very large diffs may take longer to process completely.

faq.question: Does this skill modify my code?
faq.answer: No. The skill explicitly states it is review only and never edits files. It only analyzes and reports findings.

faq.question: Is my code data safe with this skill?
faq.answer: Yes. This is a prompt-only skill. Code is only read locally for analysis and never sent anywhere.

faq.question: What if no plan exists for the changes?
faq.answer: The skill reviews against general best practices when no plan exists in the .plans directory.

faq.question: How is this different from static analysis tools?
faq.answer: This skill provides human judgment on architecture, design decisions, and plan compliance that automated tools cannot assess.
